# Strategic Doctrine

## Core doctrine

```text
Do not optimise videos for “the algorithm.”
Optimise videos for the viewer behaviours the algorithm can observe.
```

The behaviours:

```text
stop
watch
finish
rewatch
save
send
comment
follow
search
return
```

## Signal-first production

Before making a video, choose the primary signal:

```text
This video is designed to:
[ ] stop scroll
[ ] retain attention
[ ] get saved
[ ] get sent
[ ] get comments
[ ] get follows
[ ] rank in search
[ ] build trust
```

Then choose tactics that move that signal.

## Tactical doctrine

```text
First frame earns the view.
Hook earns the next 3 seconds.
First payoff earns the body.
Proof earns trust.
Structure earns completion.
Utility earns saves.
Identity earns sends.
Prompt earns comments.
Series earns follows.
Search alignment earns long tail.
```

## What to stop doing

```text
Stop treating hashtags as strategy.
Stop using #FYP as a growth plan.
Stop posting volume without experiments.
Stop opening with context before tension.
Stop using heavy central text over busy footage.
Stop measuring views alone.
Stop copying viral videos without identifying the mechanism.
Stop using “follow for more” as the default CTA.
Stop mistaking fast cuts for story movement.
Stop relying on generic AI/stock/template workflows without original proof or judgment.
```

## What to do instead

```text
Design first frame before script.
Write 5 hooks for every idea.
Choose one primary action per video.
Show payoff early.
Use text only when it clarifies.
Add proof wherever possible.
Create story movement, not visual noise.
Use platform-native packaging.
Track 24h, 72h, 7d, and 30d performance.
Scale mechanisms, not viral accidents.
```
